behavior_summary: U8P_FLOUR::look is the object's 'U8P_FLOUR::look' (examine) handler. When the player examines the FLOUR object it presents a short description to the player: the text "sack of flour".
After showing the description the process issues a suspend/exit, so the U8P_FLOUR::look handling ends immediately after the bark.
There are no branches, menus, flag reads/writes, or external calls in this snippet — behavior is a simple one-step description display.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: enter U8P_FLOUR::look process -> display Bark ('sack of flour') -> suspend (end) the process.
No conditional branches or alternative paths are present; the function is deterministic for the U8P_FLOUR::look event.
open_questions: Does the 'suspend' token here map to a full process termination or to a temporary yield in the engine's coroutine model (in practice it appears to end this U8P_FLOUR::look interaction)?
Is this object referenced by other code paths (use/interaction handlers) elsewhere in the game, or is it strictly a static descriptive object?

main_function.name: U8P_FLOUR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description for the FLOUR object when the player examines it (display 'sack of flour') and then end/suspend the U8P_FLOUR::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player performing the 'U8P_FLOUR::look' (examine) action on the FLOUR object.
